description: Lock Bellows to source-backed Iron Age pot-bellows evidence while keeping unsupported prehistoric leather-and-wood and basic-smelting claims out of the graph.

checks.reason: The launch-quality date is anchored to the Abel Beth Maacah Iron IB pot-bellows context rather than the old Ancient era-default placeholder.
The first-known source-backed form is a ceramic pot-bellows assemblage, so pottery is the sourced material path without claiming every bellows form requires ceramics.
The previous woodworking edge was sourced only to unrelated Pleistocene wooden artifacts, not bellows or metalworking forced draft.
The launch-quality source does not document a leather bag or hide membrane dependency for the first-known pot-bellows evidence.
Earliest copper smelting evidence predates securely sourced bellows, so forced-draft bellows must not become a hard prerequisite for basic smelting.
